meaning_english: Brisk and cheerful readiness. Synonyms: Eagerness, Willingness, Readiness, Promptness.
Deviating from what is standard or normal. Synonyms: Abnormal, Irregular, Peculiar, Atypical.
To make an unpleasant feeling less intense. Synonyms: Relieve, Alleviate, Mitigate, Soothe.
To reprimand someone severely. Synonyms: Reprimand, Chastise, Scold, Censure.
To feel drained of energy or vitality. Synonyms: Exhaust, Fatigue, Weaken, Devitalize.
Lasting for a very short time. Synonyms: Transitory, Transient, Fleeting, Momentary.
Open to more than one interpretation; ambiguous. Synonyms: Ambiguous, Vague, Uncertain, Obscure.
Very attentive to and concerned about accuracy and detail. Synonyms: Scrupulous, Punctilious, Demanding, Finicky.
In an initial stage; beginning to happen. Synonyms: Nascent, Emerging, Developing, Dawning.
Using very few words. Synonyms: Brief, Concise, Terse, Succinct.
Tending to talk a great deal. Synonyms: Talkative, Garrulous, Voluble, Chatty.
Generous or forgiving, especially toward a rival. Synonyms: Generous, Charitable, Benevolent, Noble.
To appease the anger or anxiety of someone. Synonyms: Appease, Placate, Pacify, Calm.
Stubbornly refusing to change one's opinion. Synonyms: Stubborn, Obstinate, Unyielding, Adamant.
Spreading widely throughout an area or group. Synonyms: Ubiquitous, Prevalent, Widespread, Omnipresent.
To speak or act in an evasive way to avoid the truth. Synonyms: Equivocate, Hedge, Evade, Fib.
Showing a lack of courage or determination. Synonyms: Cowardly, Timid, Fearful, Spineless.
Not revealing one's thoughts or feelings readily. Synonyms: Reserved, Withdrawn, Introverted, Shy.
Optimistic or positive, especially in a bad situation. Synonyms: Optimistic, Hopeful, Confident, Cheerful.
To waver between different opinions or actions. Synonyms: Hesitate, Waver, Dither, Teeter.
To make something bad better. Synonyms: Improve, Better, Enhance, Amend.
Clear, logical, and convincing. Synonyms: Convincing, Persuasive, Compelling, Strong.
Temporarily inactive or in a deep sleep. Synonyms: Inactive, Sleeping, Latent, Quiescent.
Show or declare that someone is not guilty. Synonyms: Absolve, Acquit, Vindicate, Clear.
Excessively talkative, especially on trivial matters. Synonyms: Talkative, Loquacious, Chatty, Verbose.
Having little or no money. Synonyms: Penniless, Poor, Destitute, Indigent.
Overwhelm someone with things or people to be dealt with. Synonyms: Overwhelm, Flood, Swamp, Overrun.
Make less severe, serious, or painful. Synonyms: Alleviate, Reduce, Diminish, Lessen.
The presence of something only in small or insufficient quantities. Synonyms: Scarcity, Dearth, Shortage, Lack.
Regard with great respect; revere. Synonyms: Revere, Worship, Respect, Honor.
